Site.name: HELM Studio
Site.tagline: Bespoke AI automations for European creators.

Hero.title: We build [bespoke] [AI] automations for content creators.
Hero.footnote: You pay only if it actually saved you time.

HowItWorks.title: The [two-week] pilot.
HowItWorks.intro: No abstract quotes. No signed commitment. We build an automation stitched to your workflow, you use it free, you pay only if it actually saved you time on a real video.
HowItWorks.step01Title: A call with a technical lead
HowItWorks.step01Body: We figure out together which recurring task drains you most: setup, research, repurposing, descriptions, sponsor matching… We pick the starting point with you, not for you.
HowItWorks.step02Body: Our technical team prepares the automation tailored to your specific flow — not a generic template, a solution stitched to the channel you actually run.
HowItWorks.step03Body: You try the automation on a real video, end to end. At the end of the week, you decide: if it saved you time, you pay a flat fee to keep it. Otherwise, no hard feelings.

WhatWeBuild.title: Not a catalogue. A [sample book].
WhatWeBuild.intro: Every pilot starts from something concrete. Tap an automation below to see how we think about it — not generic templates, solutions stitched to your specific workflow.

items.label: Transcript → Notion
items.problem: Your video transcripts are searchable assets for the future, but copying them into Notion by hand is something you never actually do.
items.solution: Webhook on new YouTube upload → transcript extraction (auto or human-edited if available) → automated creation of a Notion page with sections, highlighted quotes, and niche tags.
items.example: From a 25-minute video to a Notion page with 8 chapters and 4 searchable quotes in under 60 seconds.

items.label: B-roll search
items.problem: You're looking for a specific shot from your old videos and end up scrubbing for 20 minutes without finding it.
items.solution: We index your entire library (frame + audio) in a vector database. You search by voice: "when I soldered the red PCB" → exact timestamps returned.
items.example: 280 videos indexed. Find a precise scene in 3 seconds instead of 20 minutes of scrubbing.

items.label: Thumbnail A/B
items.problem: The wrong thumbnail costs views, but you have no time to test 5 variants on every upload.
items.solution: We generate 5–10 variants in your style (text, crop, subject), test them via the YouTube experiments API, and report only the winner with numbers.
items.example: 7 thumbnails tested in the 48h after upload. Keep it or swap it with one click.

items.label: Sponsor matching
items.problem: The right brands for your channel rarely reach out. The wrong ones email you every week.
items.solution: RAG on your channel + audience analysis → a dynamic media kit. We identify brands whose products match your content and prepare the pitch email in your tone.
items.example: 6 brands identified in the retro-tech niche, 3 positive replies in 2 weeks.

items.label: Description writer
items.problem: Writing description + chapters + tags + affiliate links on every video is 20 repetitive minutes you avoid.
items.solution: Pipeline that takes transcript + metadata and generates SEO description in your tone, chapters with timestamps, optimised YouTube tags, and a filled affiliate block.
items.example: From raw video to publishable description in 90 seconds instead of 20 minutes.

items.label: Chapter generator
items.problem: Chapters lift retention but adding them by hand is the first thing you skip when you're in a hurry.
items.solution: LLM analyses the video structure from transcript and audio, proposes chapters with titles in your tone and exact timestamps. You approve or tweak.
items.example: 12 chapters generated in 30s on a 25-minute video, titles written like you'd write them.

items.label: Title testing
items.problem: You have 3 titles in mind, you don't know which one performs best, and you don't want to be a YouTube growth guy.
items.solution: We generate 5 variants in your style (zero clickbait), test via YouTube experiments, report the best performer with data.
items.example: 5 titles on a video, the side-pick you wouldn't have chosen wins: +22% CTR vs original.
items.timeSaved: Measurable CTR uplift, ~10 min per upload

items.label: Shorts editor
items.problem: Cutting 3 Shorts from each long-form is hours of editing that often gets skipped entirely.
items.solution: LLM finds the most clip-worthy moments using retention curve + transcript, generates the cut, zoom, and captions. You approve the top 3.
items.example: 8 Shorts ready from a 25-minute long-form. Publish or discard in a few minutes.

items.label: Setup VM legacy
items.problem: Every time you bring back a vintage card you lose a whole night installing the right OS and drivers.
items.solution: Pre-configured VM/imaging library (Win 98 SE, XP, driver variants). Single command to spin up a test environment.
items.example: VMAS test ready in 90 seconds, no more Ctrl+A spammed until 4am.

items.label: Outlier research
items.problem: Essay-style videos take hours of research across academic PDFs, Substack newsletters, OECD reports that you cite by hand each time.
items.solution: Custom RAG that ingests your reading list + source PDFs, generates structured summaries and thematic clusters. Every fact cited with source.
items.example: 22 sources aggregated into a 4-page brief with a timeline and thematic clusters.

items.label: Newsletter draft
items.problem: The newsletter that follows the video is 90 minutes of rewriting that drains your post-upload energy.
items.solution: Transcript → Substack draft in your tone, with sections, lead, and link to the video. You review for 15 minutes and publish.
items.example: A 1200-word newsletter ready in 4 minutes, with a quarter-hour of polish on top.

items.label: Podcast show notes
items.problem: Your podcast show notes are SEO and audience growth, but you keep pushing them off because of fatigue.
items.solution: From podcast audio → full show notes: 3 paragraphs, timestamps, key quotes, guest bio, social links.
items.example: 35 minutes of podcast → 6 chapters + 8 quotes + guest bio in 90 seconds.

items.label: Comment triage
items.problem: Under your videos you get 500 comments, 90% noise, 10% important questions you'd love to see but don't.
items.solution: We classify the comments (question, idea, fan, hater, sponsor) and send a daily digest of just the "tier 1" ones to reply to.
items.example: From 320 comments on a video, 28 flagged "reply". Answered all of them in 12 minutes.

items.label: Affiliate aggregator
items.problem: You keep 15 affiliate links in mind + an old spreadsheet + copy-paste descriptions video after video.
items.solution: Affiliate database with automatic lookup → links injected for the products mentioned in the video (transcript-based).
items.example: 14 links automatically inserted into the description of an audio teardown, with disclosure.

items.label: Reading vlog highlights
items.problem: Reading vlogs have 30 minutes of daily life and 5 strong lines about books scattered everywhere. Social audiences skip them.
items.solution: We extract the "best of" book moments from your vlogs → reel/short version with optimised captions.
items.example: 10 highlights like "why Colette is working on me" extracted from 4 vlogs.

items.label: Sponsor brief
items.problem: When a brand reaches out you need to reply with an updated media kit. The one you have is from 2024.
items.solution: Dynamic media kit (audience demo, top 10 videos, average CPM, examples of sponsor integrations) always up to date. Just forward it.
items.example: Brand asks for pricing → you send an updated PDF in 30 seconds instead of spending 1h refreshing the stats.

About.title: What [Morningside] is for companies. For creators.
About.origin: HELM Studio was founded in 2026 as the European spin-off of Morningside AI, the first agency to formalise the "build custom AI workflows" model — solutions stitched to the client, not generic templates recycled from one channel to the next.
About.differentiation: Morningside works with companies. We do the same work for people who run a channel, a podcast, a newsletter — individuals, not departments.
About.people: When we write to you, there isn't a sales rep behind the message — it's the technical team that builds the work itself. No resellers, no middle layer: you talk directly with the person whose hands are on the code.
About.manifesto: Real automation shows up on your publishing calendar, not on a sales deck.

HowItWorksPage.pageIntro: No abstract quotes. No lock-in. No resellers. We build an automation stitched to your workflow, you use it free for a week, then you decide if it's worth paying for. We work this way because we've had enough of software you buy in the dark.

steps.title: We figure out what to automate, together.
steps.body: A 30-minute call with one of the team's technical leads — not a sales rep. You describe where you lose time, we figure out whether it's actually automatable or whether it's a case for "leave it manual" (it happens, and we'll tell you). If it makes sense, we go. If not, we explain why and that's it — no pushy follow-up.
steps.youGive: 30 minutes of your time + an honest description of a recurring task.
steps.youGet: A frank evaluation: can be done, can't be done, and why.

steps.title: We build the automation.
steps.body: Week 1 (5 working days). The technical team builds the solution tailored to the specific workflow we picked. No generic templates. No "let's adapt something we already built for someone else". Your code, hosted wherever you decide, documented in your working language. Updates every 1–2 days on what's done.
steps.youGive: Access to the systems we need (test accounts, API keys, sample files if relevant). Supervised, no sensitive data.
steps.youGet: A working automation by end of week, with documentation and a 30-minute training.

steps.title: You use it free, you decide.
steps.body: Week 2 (7 days). You use it on a real production video, end to end. We're on standby for support if needed, but we don't look over your shoulder. At week's end you decide: if it saved you time, you pay a flat fee (we told you the figure before the pilot — no surprises). If not, no hard feelings.
steps.youGive: Seven days of real use + honesty at the end of the pilot.
steps.youGet: An informed decision. No lock-in, no subscription, no middle layer.
steps.timeline: 7 days, decision on the following Monday.

HowItWorksPage.pricingBody: By default, a one-off flat fee, declared before the pilot even begins. How much? It depends on the workflow's complexity — a description writer costs less than a RAG agent over 500 videos. We give you the exact number in the step 01 call, once we know what's needed. Prefer a subscription model instead? Let's talk: we can structure it that way — the choice is yours.
pricingBullets: By default a single one-off fee, declared before the pilot starts.
Subscription if you prefer — your call, not a forced default.
No lock-in: the code is yours, hosted wherever you want.
If it doesn't work, you don't pay. Period.

faq.q: What if my workflow is too specific to be automated?
faq.a: We tell you straight away. In the step 01 call we check whether there's enough structural repetition to build something on top of. If there isn't, it's an honest "no" and we're still on good terms. We don't sell you a fake automation.

faq.q: What if I'm not convinced at the end of the pilot?
faq.a: Exactly nothing happens: you don't pay, we say goodbye. You still have the code if you want to keep it (even just to try it yourself or hand it to one of your developers later). No reason needed, no pushy follow-ups.

faq.q: Can I keep working on it with my own developers after the pilot?
faq.a: Yes, the code is yours. Documentation in your working language, clean repository, no obscure magic. If you want maintenance from us later, you check with us first, but you're free to do as you like.

faq.q: What does "flat fee" mean? And can I pay as a subscription if I prefer?
faq.a: The default model is one fee when you decide to keep the automation, declared before the pilot. No per-video cost, no surprises. If you prefer a monthly or quarterly subscription, we can structure it that way — just ask. Flexibility is on your side.
Yes, before the step 01 call if you ask us. Usually it's not needed because we talk about workflows, not sensitive data or sponsor contracts, but we're fine signing one anyway.

faq.q: How technical does the workflow I bring need to be?
faq.a: From not at all to very. We've built simple automations (description scripts) and complex ones (vintage hardware test environments). We figure it out in the call: you don't need to be an engineer to talk to us.

faq.q: Is there a minimum on subscribers, views, or revenue to work with you?
faq.a: No. What matters is having a recurring workflow worth automating, not how big you are. We've worked with creators at 5k subs and at 500k. The pilot is the same for everyone.

HowItWorksPage.ctaBody: The step 01 call lasts 30 minutes, it's free, and by the end of it you know whether it makes sense to move on or not.

AboutPage.pageIntro: HELM Studio is the European spin-off of Morningside AI — the New Zealand agency that codified the "build custom AI workflows" model. They work with companies. We do the same work for people who run, in person, a channel, a podcast, a newsletter.
originParagraphs: Morningside AI was founded in Auckland in 2023. It's the agency that first formalised the idea of not selling SaaS, but building automations stitched to the client — custom code, no white-label products, no recycled templates.

Four years later, a group of European technicians brought the same approach to Europe, with one crucial difference: our clients aren't companies, they're people. Individual creators working alone or in tiny teams, who can't make sense of generic AI tools.

Since January 2026 the studio has been based in Milan, with three satellite offices (Barcelona, Berlin, Dublin) — one for each of the main markets we operate in. Not for a "remote-first" fashion, but because being in the places where our clients live means speaking their language, knowing their platforms, understanding their brand deals.
AboutPage.positioningTitle: What we [are], what we are not.
AboutPage.positioningIntro: The "AI for creators" market is full of noise. Let's set the record straight.
positioningNotItems: A social or growth marketing agency.
A SaaS provider or a vendor of recycled AI templates.
Ghostwriters, video editors, or content producers.
Resellers of Make, Zapier, n8n or similar.
positioningAreItems: A technical studio that builds custom AI automations.
Build-first: new code stitched to your specific workflow.
On the ground in Europe, with four offices and four languages.
Direct: you talk to the person whose hands are on the code.

manifestoPrinciples.title: Build before you talk.
manifestoPrinciples.body: Sales decks don't automate anything. You can tell when an automation actually works: you find it on your publishing calendar, not in a PDF.

manifestoPrinciples.title: Honesty before the sale.
manifestoPrinciples.body: If your workflow can't be automated, we tell you in the first call and that's it. Selling you something fake is the worst thing we could do for our reputation.

manifestoPrinciples.title: The code is yours.
manifestoPrinciples.body: No lock-in, no proprietary SaaS, no "cancel and you lose everything". At the end of the pilot you have a clean repository and documentation you can hand to anyone.

manifestoPrinciples.title: Time, not clicks.
manifestoPrinciples.body: We don't sell you views, engagement or social growth. We sell you hours that come back onto your calendar, to spend on what made you a creator in the first place.

AboutPage.officesIntro: One person physically present in each of the four cities where we operate. Not ideological remote-first — proximity matters in brand deals, in payments, in understanding local markets.

offices.note: Legal HQ, Italian operating base, and administrative heart of the studio.
Covers the Spanish-speaking creator community and Iberian brands.
Covers DACH creators — Germany, Austria, German-speaking Switzerland.
Covers UK + IE creators and English-language sponsor relations.

AboutPage.ctaTitle: Want to see if it makes sense to [talk]?
AboutPage.ctaBody: The step 01 call lasts 30 minutes and by the end you know whether to move on or not. No commitment.

Contact.title: [Let's talk.]
Contact.intro: Tell us what drains you most in your workflow. A person from the technical team replies within one business day. No sales reps, no form-bots.

form.channelPlaceholder: URL or handle — helps us understand context
form.messagePlaceholder: A recurring task that eats your hours. No need to be precise — we'll start from there together.
form.successBody: We'll write back to the address you gave us within one business day.
form.errorBody: Try again in a moment, or write directly to {email}.

NotFound.title: Site under [demolition].
NotFound.body: This page isn't here. Either it never was, or we moved it, or there's a typo in the URL. It happens.
NotFound.ctaContact: Write to us if you were looking for something

Footer.manifesto: We build tools that earn time, not clicks.
